description_wiki: Enumclaw (  EE-nəm-klaw) is a city in King County, Washington, United States.

description: city in King County, Washington, United States of America
description_wiki: Auburn (Lushootseed: sladə) is a city in King County, Washington, United States (with a small portion crossing into neighboring Pierce County).

description: stratovolcano in the U.S. state of Washington
description_wiki: Mount Rainier ( ray-NEER), also known as Tahoma, is a large, active stratovolcano in the Cascade Range of the Pacific Northwest in the United States.

description: capital city of Hiroshima Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Hiroshima (広島市, Hiroshima-shi; English: , also UK: , US: , Japanese: [çiɾoɕima] ) is the capital of Hiroshima Prefecture in Japan.

surface_forms: the big news on the papers
description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.
The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) is the domestic intelligence and security service of the United States and its principal federal law enforcement agency.

description: county seat of Fresno County, California, United States
description_wiki: Fresno ( ; Spanish for 'ash tree') is a city in the San Joaquin Valley of California, United States.

description: city in Santa Clara County, California, United States
description_wiki: Gilroy is a city in Santa Clara County, California, United States, south of the San Francisco Bay Area.

description: city in Fresno County, California, United States
description_wiki: Reedley is a city in Fresno County, California, United States.

description: detainee camp in Arizona, United States
description_wiki:
The Poston Internment Camp, located in Yuma County (now in La Paz County) in southwestern Arizona, was the largest (in terms of area) of the 10 American concentration camps operated by the War Relocation Authority during World War II.
The site was composed of three separate camps arranged in a chain from north to south, three miles from each other.

Nisei (二世, "second generation") is a Japanese-language term used in countries in North America and South America to specify the ethnically Japanese children born in the new country to Japanese-born immigrants, or Issei.

description: Japanese people who were the first generation of immigrants from Japan
description_wiki: Issei (一世, "first generation") are Japanese immigrants to countries in North America and South America.

surface_forms: the largest of all the concentration camps
description_wiki: The Tule Lake War Relocation Center, also known as the Tule Lake Segregation Center, was an American concentration camp located in Modoc and Siskiyou counties in California and constructed in 1942 by the United States government to incarcerate Japanese Americans, forcibly removing them from their homes on the West Coast of the United States.

Kibei (kibei (帰米; literally "go home to America")) refers to Japanese Americans born in the United States whose parents had sent them to receive their education in Japan and who had then returned to the US by the time that the US and Japan became engaged in war in the 1940s.

description: Internment camp for Japanese Americans in Millard County, Utah, United States
description_wiki: The Topaz War Relocation Center, also known as the Central Utah Relocation Center (Topaz) and briefly as the Abraham Relocation Center, was an American concentration camp in which Americans of Japanese descent and immigrants who had come to the United States from Japan, called Nikkei were incarcerated.
